capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 1-10; Two-way premium; capital reflects top-side CB/WR position; Tier A confidence
strengths: Generationally unique two-way player with elite CB and WR production in the same season — a feat not accomplished at Power 5 level in decades. 9.5 athleticism profile combines top-end speed (sub-4.4 projection), elite jump ball skills, and exceptional change-of-direction. Two-Way Premium reflects genuine draft capital elevation: whichever side he plays, he profiles as a top-10 pick at that position.
red_flags: NFL teams must commit to one position; transition risk is real regardless of skill set. Two-way college workload means fewer reps in pure CB technique fundamentals (press technique, trail technique, jam footwork) compared to a single-position prospect. COMPRESSION gap reflects his elite balance across all archetypes rather than a concern — no dominant archetype because all are elite.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2 top) / R2; Tier C — ±1 round range, monitor; system dependency discount applied
strengths: Elite touch and accuracy from the pocket, NFL-ready release mechanics, and exceptional ability to navigate pressure and extend plays within his frame. Strong statistical output at Colorado against Power 4 competition demonstrates adaptability beyond his JSU baseline. Process as a passer, specifically his pre-snap identification and quick release reads, is genuinely high-floor.
red_flags: System dependency is the primary concern — Colorado's offense was architecturally designed around Sanders' skill set under Deion Sanders' bespoke scheme. Tier C confidence reflects legitimate unknowns: independent coaching relationship (Deion factor reduces confidence in c2/c3 baseline accuracy), limited scheme-diverse reps, and questions about translating production to an NFL system he did not design. RB athleticism profile limits scramble/extend threat value significantly. Capital carries ±1 round Tier C range.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32 / R1 Top depending on team need; Tier A confidence
strengths: Young and physically imposing OT with elite athleticism for the position — long arms, exceptional anchor strength, and outstanding short-area burst that stresses interior defenders. Missouri background provided meaningful SEC-adjacent competition, and Membou dominated in both run and pass protection. Developmental trajectory is among the best in this class: already dominant at a young age with clear ceiling remaining.
red_flags: COMPRESSION gap indicates he profiles well in multiple archetypes (athletic projection and complete prospect) without a dominant singular identity — a positive signal for this class. Still developing elite hand technique and combat consistency in pass rush, which will be stress-tested in the NFL. Limited power conference playoff depth at Missouri reduces SOS confidence.

capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2 top; Tier A confidence
strengths: Elite run blocker with outstanding leverage, power, and contact-seeking mentality — every NFL run-first coordinator's ideal interior blocker. Four-year starter at Georgia with consistent dominance in SEC competition against NFL-caliber defensive linemen. Excellent competitive toughness and effort tail that elevates every snap.
red_flags: Athletic profile for an OG is solid but not exceptional; limited to run-heavy scheme alignments in full exploitation. Pass protection against elite speed rushers off the edge is his biggest projection concern — tendency to overextend hand placement under pressure. SOLID archetype gap is clean but not elite, reflecting a clear fit without the upside ceiling of the COMPRESSION tier.

capital_adjusted: R2 / R3 top; Tier A confidence
strengths: Well-rounded TE prospect with above-average athleticism and reliable hands in contested catch situations. Texas system provided meaningful opportunities against Big 12 pass rushers and coverage. Effective in both seam routes and in-line blocking, making him a viable two-way TE at the NFL level. TWEENER gap reflects balance between receiving-first and complete archetypes.
red_flags: Not an elite separator — relies on size and contested-catch ability rather than route precision or YAC creation. Blocking technique needs refinement at the point of attack against NFL-caliber defensive ends. His overall profile projects as a quality starter but not a top-5 TE in this class. Draft capital reflects good but not elite upside.

capital_adjusted: Day 3 / R4-R5 range; Tier B — ±0.5 round range; RB positional discount applied
strengths: Good short-area quickness and burst behind pads, effective in zone-read and inside zone concepts. Georgia production reflects good efficiency when healthy and playing behind an elite offensive line. Legitimate pass-catching ability from the backfield is a positive NFL-era trait.
red_flags: NO_FIT archetype gap reflects a profile that doesn't dominate any specific RB archetype — not elite enough to anchor as a feature back, not specialized enough to be a true satellite/receiving back, not physical enough as a between-the-tackles specialist. PVC of 0.70 (lowest positional premium) reflects market reality. Tier B confidence due to transfer context and inconsistent production volume. DEVELOPMENTAL tier is appropriate given the current trait-to-production ceiling alignment; projection requires scheme-specific development.

capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2 top; SOS Gate note — production confidence Tier B; ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite athletic profile for the safety position — outstanding speed, range, and zone coverage ability that projects to premium box safety or single-high roles. South Carolina production showed consistent effort and playmaking against SEC competition. Physical profile and processing speed make him a legitimate early-round safety in the post-Ed Reed era of the position.
red_flags: SOS Gate applied: South Carolina schedule strength in 2025 did not include consistent elite competition, creating uncertainty in the production-quality floor. v_production discounted from raw assessment by -1.5 to reflect this. Tier B confidence due to SOS limitations. COMPRESSION gap reflects a complete safety profile without a singular dominant trait — athleticism leads but processing and versatility are nearly equal; this is a positive signal, not a concern.

capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2; Tier A confidence; production-led profile
strengths: Elite production from the interior at Ohio State against Big Ten competition — consistently penetrated against NFL offensive linemen and generated wins both against the run and in pass rush. Exceptional competitive toughness; does not take snaps off, high motor in pursuit. Athleticism profile for an IDL is above average with a quick first step and leverage advantage.
red_flags: SOLID archetype gap (not COMPRESSION) reflects a player who is clearly a Production Machine without elite versatility or developmental upside ceiling. IDL PVC of 0.90 reflects positional market reality. Route to elite production in the NFL requires continued counter-move development — currently relies heavily on power and leverage rather than diverse pass rush plan.

capital_adjusted: R2 / R3 top; Tier A confidence; C positional discount reflected
strengths: Elite center intelligence — outstanding pre-snap communication, protection call identification, and line calls make him a natural for NFL green-dot center duties. Pittsburgh background developed a complete center skill set across both run and pass protection. Processing speed at the center position is among the best in the 2026 class; veteran coaches consistently praise his film study.
red_flags: Athletic profile is serviceable but below elite for the position — lacks the burst to pull effectively in stretch schemes or reach block against explosive 3-techs. C PVC of 0.80 reflects positional market reality; centers rarely command premium capital regardless of quality. Production at Pitt (ACC competition) did not consistently stress-test against Big Ten or SEC interior talent.

capital_adjusted: R2 / R3; Tier A confidence; C positional discount; production-led profile
strengths: Dominant run blocker at Georgia against elite SEC competition — powered the Bulldogs' run game with consistent positional dominance at the point of attack. Outstanding physical profile for the center position with excellent anchor strength and hand power. SEC championship experience provides elite competition floor confidence.
red_flags: Processing and protection call intelligence is good but below elite — projects more as a run-first center than a pure green-dot communicator, which limits upside in pass-heavy schemes. C PVC of 0.80 reflects market reality. Development in pass protection technique (hand replacement, set mechanics under speed) is the key NFL translation variable.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2)
strengths: Elite athleticism with 9.84 RAS creates exceptional versatility in coverage and pass rush situations. Strong competitive toughness and motor translate to consistent effort across all phases. Scheme versatility allows deployment in multiple alignments with effectiveness in both run fits and coverage assignments.
red_flags: Production metrics lag behind athletic profile, suggesting inconsistent impact despite elite physical tools. Processing development still ongoing with occasional missed assignments in complex coverage concepts. Limited sample size of elite competition exposure raises questions about ceiling projection.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) — Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite pre-snap processor who consistently diagnoses offensive formations and communicates protection adjustments to teammates. Shows excellent instincts in zone coverage and demonstrates consistent tackle technique with strong competitive drive in big-game situations.
red_flags: Athletic limitations may restrict coverage range against elite receiving backs and tight ends in the NFL. Limited pass rush upside and questions about maintaining production level against faster, more athletic competition at the next level.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite athleticism with 9.87 RAS translates to exceptional range and coverage ability in space. Shows strong versatility as both coverage linebacker and blitz weapon with developing anticipation skills. Physical tools suggest significant upside with proper coaching development.
red_flags: Production inconsistencies suggest processing speed still developing relative to elite athletic tools. Limited exposure to complex NFL-level route combinations and protection schemes. Tier B confidence reflects need for more complete evaluation against top-tier competition.

capital_adjusted: R1 top-10; EDGE premium; ELITE composite; Tier A confidence
strengths: Elite first-step quickness and bend around the arc; projects as an immediate Day 1 starter at a premium position. Pass-rush win rate against Power 4 tackles is elite. Motor is exceptional — plays through whistle and chases the ball consistently. Athleticism profile is top-tier for the position.
red_flags: Power-to-counter game requires NFL refinement — bend-heavy rushers occasionally struggle when tackles take away the arc. Production at Miami (FL) carries some recency bias from elevated team winning environment. Injury history must be clean-confirmed before top-10 capital.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 1-10
strengths: Elite athletic specimen with 9.99 RAS who combines freakish physical tools with advanced pre-snap processing and diagnostic ability. His versatility allows deployment across multiple linebacker positions while maintaining elite coverage ability against tight ends and running backs. Natural leader with excellent instincts who can quarterback the defense from the middle linebacker spot.
red_flags: Questions about consistent gap discipline and occasional over-pursuit on run plays when relying on athleticism over assignment. Limited exposure as primary signal caller in Ohio State's defensive system may create adjustment period at next level. Some concerns about maintaining weight and strength for every-down role against NFL-caliber offensive lines.

capital_adjusted: R3 Tier B — ±0.5 round range

red_flags: Below-average athleticism significantly limits coverage range and pass defense versatility. Processing speed can be inconsistent on complex passing concepts. Limited scheme versatility may restrict NFL deployment to run-heavy packages.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) - Tier B — ±0.5 round range

red_flags: Limited range in coverage situations and struggles to match athletic tight ends and running backs in space. Processing speed against play-action and misdirection needs improvement. Lacks the elite athleticism to develop into a three-down linebacker at the NFL level.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range

red_flags: Processing speed can be inconsistent on complex play-action concepts and RPO reads. Limited experience in diverse coverage packages beyond basic zone responsibilities. Tier B confidence due to some gaps in high-level competition exposure.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32
strengths: Elite pre-snap processor with exceptional field vision and quick decision-making under pressure. capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32
strengths: Elite processing speed and pre-snap recognition allow him to diagnose plays instantly and make correct coverage rotations. Exceptional range and ball skills make him a true centerfield presence who can erase deep routes. Outstanding competitive toughness shown in big games with clutch interceptions and physical run support despite his frame.
red_flags: Lacks elite straight-line speed compared to premier safety prospects, which could limit his ceiling against vertical NFL offenses. Physical development needed to handle bigger tight ends and slot receivers in coverage. Limited experience against true elite quarterback play in college could present adjustment challenges early in NFL career.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32
strengths: Elite physical tools with exceptional motor and competitive drive that translates to dominant production against high-level competition. Demonstrates complete skill set as both pass rusher and run defender with advanced hand technique and bend for his size. Natural feel for leverage and timing with the burst to convert speed to power consistently.
red_flags: Limited sample size at highest level with questions about sustained consistency over full college career. Technique refinement needed in counter package development to maximize pass rush ceiling. May need time to adjust to increased strength and athleticism of NFL offensive tackles.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32 - RB positional value limits ceiling despite elite traits
strengths: Elite combination of size, speed, and vision with proven ability to carry heavy workloads at Notre Dame. Exceptional contact balance and tackle-breaking ability through traffic, plus legitimate receiving skills out of the backfield. Natural runner with great feel for cutback lanes and setting up blocks.
red_flags: Limited pass protection experience and technique refinement needed at NFL level. Some durability concerns with heavy collegiate usage pattern. Positional value coefficient at 0.70x creates structural draft capital limitations despite elite skill set.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32, Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite first-step quickness and burst off the line creates immediate separation from offensive tackles. Natural bend around the corner with exceptional hip flexibility allows him to maintain rush lanes while dipping under blockers. High motor player who consistently finishes plays and shows excellent effort in pursuit.
red_flags: Limited hand technique development means he relies heavily on athleticism to win reps. Needs to add functional strength and counter moves to handle power rushers who can reset the pocket. Run defense consistency needs improvement as he can get washed out by physical blocking schemes.

capital_adjusted: R1 Top (early) - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite physical tools with outstanding length and speed create constant disruption in coverage. Shows exceptional competitive toughness in big games and demonstrates consistent technique improvement year over year. High-character player with strong work ethic and coachability.
red_flags: Processing still developing as he relies more on athletic ability than anticipation. Limited sample size against elite receiver competition. Technique can break down when pressed by high-level route runners, though trajectory is encouraging.

capital_adjusted: R1 Top (early)

red_flags: Limited elite breakaway speed may cap big-play potential against NFL corners. Relatively modest production numbers for a consensus top receiver. Questions about ceiling against elite press-man coverage at the next level.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 12-22, Tier B-Conditional — Man coverage floor: confirmed, FM-4 suppression active, combine medical clearance required
strengths: Elite anticipatory processing with confirmed CB-1 mechanism allows him to break on routes before receivers complete their stems. Dominant press-man coverage technique with physical tools to match elite receivers at the catch point. Exceptional competitive toughness and assignment execution validated against SEC competition.
red_flags: January 2025 ACL tear resulted in missed entire 2025 season, creating significant medical risk and movement quality questions. Post-injury mobility and explosion must be confirmed at combine medical evaluations. Extended recovery timeline may impact draft timing and early career availability.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite hand technique and counter sequencing off initial punch creates consistent pressure through technical precision rather than athleticism. Strong competitive toughness and injury durability with clean character profile. Scheme versatile with confirmed three-counter package that translates across multiple fronts.
red_flags: Limited elite athleticism compared to top EDGE prospects may create ceiling concerns against top-tier offensive tackles. Development trajectory appears steady but not explosive, suggesting current production may be near his ceiling. Tier B confidence due to limited exposure against elite competition in SEC play.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite physical profile with impressive length and athleticism for the tackle position. Shows natural movement skills and has demonstrated ability to handle top-tier SEC competition. High developmental ceiling with proper coaching and technique refinement in NFL system.
red_flags: Processing speed and technical consistency need significant improvement. Transfer history raises questions about commitment and coachability. Production has been inconsistent relative to physical tools, suggesting gaps in technique and football IQ that need addressing.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Banks shows solid penetrating ability with good first-step quickness and hand usage to create disruption in the backfield. He demonstrates versatility to play multiple alignments along the defensive line and has shown improvement in his pass rush technique throughout his Florida career. His motor runs hot and he pursues plays sideline to sideline with good effort.
red_flags: Inconsistent gap discipline in run defense can lead to big plays allowed up the middle. Pass rush moves are still developing and he relies heavily on initial penetration rather than having a refined counter move package. Competition level in recent seasons has been mixed, making it difficult to project against elite NFL offensive lines.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite physical projection with exceptional length and athletic foundation that translates well to NFL pass protection schemes. Shows natural anchor ability against power rushers and has demonstrated steady development trajectory throughout his Georgia career. Raw tools suggest significant upside with proper NFL coaching and technique refinement.
red_flags: Processing speed against elite speed rushers remains developmental, particularly in pass sets against athletic edge defenders. Scheme versatility is limited primarily to power/gap concepts with zone blocking still requiring significant work. Hand placement consistency and technique refinement needed before ready for immediate NFL starting role.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range

red_flags: Processing speed against elite edge rushers remains inconsistent, particularly in speed-to-power conversion situations. capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) - Tier B ±0.5 round range

red_flags: Limited top-end speed creates ceiling concerns against NFL defensive backs. Lacks explosive vertical separation ability which may limit big-play potential. Modest YAC production suggests limitations creating after the catch, particularly against physical coverage.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2)

red_flags: Limited elite athletic upside with concerns about recovery speed against top-tier edge rushers. Questions about ceiling given pedestrian measurable projection. May struggle with the most explosive NFL pass rushers without significant technique refinement.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range

red_flags: Limited deep speed and explosion may restrict his role to underneath routes and slot work at the next level. capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite athletic foundation with impressive length and speed that allows him to match receivers vertically and recover from suboptimal positioning. red_flags: Processing limitations show up as late breaks on routes and susceptibility to double moves when receivers sell their stems effectively. Inconsistent technique in press coverage and struggles with precise route recognition against polished route runners. May need time to develop instincts and anticipation at NFL level.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2)

red_flags: Limited hand technique development and counter moves when initial rush is stalled by tackles. Needs to add functional strength for power moves and can be susceptible to being washed out by larger offensive tackles. Production has been somewhat inconsistent despite athletic ability.
